compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Symptom
After a ticket advances from the planning to the execution plan-review phase (via
`claim`, which sets `plan_review_phase=execution`), running `rebar review-plan <id>`
— even with `--force` — refreshes the ORIGINAL planning-phase attestation instead of
minting a fresh execution-phase one. The plan-review CLOSE gate then rejects the close
with `incompatible-phase: planning -> execution (incompatible)`, and there is no
non-escape-hatch way to produce the execution attestation the close requires.

## Root cause
`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py`: the idempotence short-circuit is guarded by
`if sign and not force:` (line ~581), but the progressive drift-refresh immediately
below is guarded only by `if sign:` (line ~590) — it omits `and not force`. The
docstring/comment at line ~580 explicitly states "`force` bypasses BOTH this skip and
the drift-refresh, forcing a full re-review", so the code contradicts its own
documented contract. `orchestrator.drift_refresh` re-signs `cand["manifest"]` (the
prior planning manifest) via `attest.refresh_attestation`, preserving `review-phase:
planning`; it never reconsiders phase against the ticket's now-`execution` state.

## Trigger conditions
The prior (planning) attestation is stale ONLY by code drift (ticket material +
criteria registry unchanged) — e.g. the planning review was signed at one sha and the
implementation later landed at another — so `drift_refresh_candidate` matches and the
probe passes. Observed live landing ticket f5c0-46ce-fab1-487a: planning review signed
at sha b5585d65 (main checkout), code merged at f48a2fb41; post-claim `review-plan
--force` returned `coverage.drift_refresh=true` with a planning manifest, and
`transition in_progress closed` failed `incompatible-phase`.

## Proposed fix
Change the drift-refresh guard to `if sign and not force:` so `--force` performs a full
re-review (honoring the documented contract and minting an execution-phase attestation).
Alternatively/additionally, `drift_refresh` should decline (return None) when the
ticket's current phase has advanced past the candidate manifest's phase, so an
execution-phase ticket never refreshes a planning-phase attestation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] On an `in_progress` ticket whose only-valid attestation is planning-phase and
      whose reviewed code has drifted, `rebar review-plan <id> --force` runs a full
      review (no drift-refresh) and signs an execution-phase attestation.
- [ ] After that, `transition <id> in_progress closed` passes the plan-review close
      gate (no `incompatible-phase`).
- [ ] A regression test covers the planning-attestation + code-drift + phase-advance
      close path (extends tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_policy_dogfood.py).
- [ ] The line ~580 comment and the code guard agree.

## File impact
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py (drift-refresh force guard)
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py (optional: phase-advance decline)
- tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_policy_dogfood.py (regression)
compiled_state.title: plan-review --force does not bypass drift-refresh; execution-phase attestation cannot be minted after planning review + code drift, blocking close
